# Illimis Therapeutics Pipeline Scout Report: **ILM01 / GAIA-Aβ**

본 리포트는 SKBP Pipeline Finder v3.0 Markdown report + JSON schema 지침에 맞춰 작성했습니다. 최종에는 대시보드에 복사 가능한 JSON을 포함했고, 감사 가능성을 위해 URL과 evidence trail을 함께 남겼습니다.

---

## 1) Company Profile

| Field | Content | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Company | Illimis Therapeutics | Official company site [1]; 회사 공식 페이지에서 “Illimis Therapeutics, Inc.” 및 연락처 확인 가능 |
| Legal name / aliases | Illimis Therapeutics, Inc. / 일리미스테라퓨틱스 | Official overview/contact [2], [3] |
| Country | Republic of Korea | Seoul 주소가 공식 contact page에 기재 |
| Headquarters | Seoul, Korea. Boston presence: Lilly Gateway Labs, 15 Necco St, Boston | Official contact page [3] |
| Website | Illimis official site | [1] |
| Company type / stage | Private, Series B, preclinical-stage biotech | 공식 overview는 2021년 설립 및 fundraising 66.4M USD를 표시하고, 2025년 PR은 KRW 58B / 약 USD 42M Series B를 발표 |
| Focus areas | CNS / neurodegenerative disease / neuro-immune disease / immune disease | 공식 platform은 neurodegenerative disorders 및 amyloidosis biologics 개발을 설명하고, Series B PR은 CNS 및 immune disease development를 명시 |
| Platform summary | **GAIA: Gas6-mediated Anti-Inflammatory Adaptor**. Amyloid/pathologic target binder + engineered Gas6 TAM receptor binder를 결합한 fusion protein platform | Official platform page [5] |
| Financing / partnership signals | 2025 Series B KRW 58B; Lilly Catalyze360-ExploR&D collaboration; BMS Innovation Square, JLABS Singapore 등 | PRNewswire 및 BioSpectator |
| Lead pipeline summary | **ILM01 / GAIA-Aβ**, Aβ-targeting Alzheimer’s disease asset. 2H 2025 preclinical entry, 2027 IND/Phase 1 preparation target로 공개자료에 언급 | Official pipeline + BioSpectator/KBR |

---

## 2) Pipeline Snapshot

| Field | Content | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Company | Illimis Therapeutics | Official site [1], [2] |
| Lead asset | **ILM01 / GAIA-Aβ** | Official pipeline lists Aβ / Alzheimer’s disease / ILM01 |
| Target | Aβ aggregates + TAM receptor-mediated clearance biology | Official platform explains target binder + engineered Gas6 TAM binder architecture |
| Theme / Cluster | **Theme: Neuroimmune / Cluster: 교세포 향상성** | Internal SKBP mapping. 근거는 glial cell TAM receptor, microglia/astrocyte phagocytosis, anti-inflammatory clearance |
| MoA | Aβ binder와 engineered Gas6를 연결해 TAM receptor-dependent phagocytosis를 유도하고 FcγR-mediated inflammation을 회피 | Nature Medicine / KAIST record |
| Modality / Platform | Biologic, chimeric / bispecific-like fusion protein, GAIA platform | Official platform page |
| Indication | Alzheimer’s disease | Official pipeline |
| Stage | Public source 기준 **preclinical / IND-enabling preparation**. BioSpectator는 2025년 하반기 preclinical entry, 2027년 말 IND expectation을 언급. KBR은 2027년 Phase 1 준비 중이라고 설명 | BioSpectator + KBR |
| Key data | αAβ-Gas6가 AD/CAA mouse model에서 Aβ plaque 제거, NF-κB inflammatory response 감소, microglia/astrocyte phagocytosis, reduced synapse elimination/microhemorrhage를 보임 | Nature Medicine / KAIST / poster |

---

## 3) Scorecard Summary

| Criterion | Score | One-line judgment | Evidence used |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Target Relevance | 3 / 3 | Aβ 단독이 아니라 TAM receptor–glial phagocytosis–anti-inflammatory clearance 축이므로 Neuroimmune fit 강함 | [4], [5], [8] |
| Competitive Landscape | 1 / 3 | True same-MoA는 적지만 AD/Aβ commercial landscape는 Leqembi, Kisunla, remternetug로 과밀 | FDA/Lilly sources |
| MoA Validity | 3 / 3 | Nature Medicine publication과 poster data가 mechanistic consistency를 강하게 뒷받침 | [7], [8], [10] |
| Platform Attractiveness | 2 / 3 | Differentiated platform이나 biologic fusion protein이라 SKBP major modality fit은 중간 | [5], [13] |
| Expansion Potential | 3 / 3 | ILM02 tau, ILM03 α-syn, ILM21 MS, ILM24 SLE 등 platform 확장성 명확 | [4], [5], [13] |
| Data Maturity | 2 / 3 | Publication-backed PoC는 강하지만 final candidate GLP/CMC/clinical data는 미공개 | [8], [10], [12] |
| Marketability | 2 / 3 | A. TAP 약 46만 명, B. Unrisked Peak Sales 약 $1.94B, C. Obtainable Peak Sales 약 $1.06B 추정 | [21], [22], [19], [20] |
| **Total** | **16 / 21** | **Watch / DD priority** |  |

---

## 4) Criterion Detail Pages

### 4.1 Target Relevance

Score: **3 / 3**

Main line:  
ILM01은 Aβ-targeting AD asset이지만, 차별화 포인트는 **TAM receptor-mediated glial phagocytosis + inflammation control**이므로 SKBP Theme 기준 **Neuroimmune / 교세포 향상성**에 정확히 들어간다.

What was checked:
- Target identity
- Disease/biology relevance
- SKBP Theme / Cluster fit
- General neurodegeneration / neuroinflammation relevance

Evidence trail:
- Illimis 공식 pipeline은 Neurology 영역에서 Aβ / Alzheimer’s disease / ILM01을 명시한다.
- 공식 platform page는 TAM receptor가 glial cell surface에 발현되고 phagocytosis activation 및 inflammation inhibition과 연결된다고 설명한다.
- Nature Medicine/KAIST record는 αAβ–Gas6가 microglia와 astrocyte phagocytosis를 활성화하고 inflammatory response를 줄였다고 요약한다.

Investigation note:  
Aβ만 보면 “general neurodegeneration” 수준이지만, ILM01의 실제 differentiation은 **glial immune clearance**에 있으므로 3점으로 판단했다.

---

### 4.2 Competitive Landscape

Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
직접적으로 TAM/Gas6-based Aβ fusion protein 경쟁자는 뚜렷하지 않지만, **commercial indication 기준으로 anti-Aβ AD 시장은 이미 승인 제품 2개와 Phase III 후속 자산이 존재**한다.

What was checked:
- Same disease competitors
- Same target competitors
- Same or similar MoA competitors
- Front runner count
- Approved / Phase 3 / clinical / preclinical status

Competitor table:

| Competitor | Company | Modality | Target / MoA | Stage | Why it matters | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Leqembi / lecanemab | Eisai / Biogen | mAb | Amyloid beta-directed antibody | FDA traditional approval | Anti-Aβ class benchmark. Efficacy, ARIA, infusion burden, pricing 기준점 | [15] |
| Kisunla / donanemab | Eli Lilly | mAb | Amyloid plaque-targeting antibody | FDA approved | 두 번째 approved Aβ therapy. Limited-duration dosing 및 ARIA profile이 ILM01 positioning 기준 | [16], [17] |
| Remternetug | Eli Lilly | mAb | Aβ-targeting antibody | Phase III | ILM01 상업화 전 next-gen anti-Aβ class를 더 crowded하게 만들 수 있음 | [18] |
| Aduhelm / aducanumab | Biogen / Eisai historical | mAb | Aβ-directed antibody | Discontinued | Public αAβ-Gas6 prototype이 aducanumab scFv를 사용. 실패 사례로 commercial/evidence risk 상기 | [23] |
| XPro1595 | INmune Bio | Protein therapeutic | Selective soluble TNF neutralization | Phase II / Phase 2b-3 planning | Same disease + neuroimmune/inflammation positioning. Target은 다르지만 theme-level competitor | [25] |
| Diranersen / BIIB080 | Biogen / Ionis | ASO | MAPT mRNA reduction / tau lowering | Phase II; registrational development planned | Same AD disease-modifying competition. ILM02 tau expansion의 benchmark | [24] |

Investigation note:  
Same-MoA 경쟁은 낮다. 하지만 BD 판단에서는 실제 처방/환자/가격/임상 인프라를 공유하는 **Aβ disease-modifying therapy market**이 핵심이므로, Leqembi/Kisunla/remternetug 존재를 반영해 1점으로 두었다.

---

### 4.3 MoA Validity

Score: **3 / 3**

Main line:  
αAβ-Gas6의 MoA는 publication 기반으로 매우 탄탄하다. Aβ binding, TAM receptor activation, glial phagocytosis, reduced inflammatory signaling, reduced synapse elimination/microhemorrhage가 하나의 mechanistic chain으로 연결된다.

What was checked:
- Journal publication / PMID / DOI
- Mechanistic consistency
- Functional readout
- Disease linkage
- Safety-relevant signal

Evidence trail:
- Nature Medicine 2022 논문은 αAβ–Gas6가 TAM receptor-dependent phagocytosis를 통해 Aβ plaque를 제거하고 NF-κB inflammatory response나 reactive gliosis를 유도하지 않았다고 보고한다.
- 같은 record는 AD 및 CAA model mice에서 behavioral outcome 개선, synapse elimination 및 microhemorrhage 감소를 요약한다.
- Illimis Keystone poster는 HMC3 uptake, THP-1Axl cytokine/NF-κB reporter, primary glia uptake, 5XFAD in vivo plaque reduction, scRNA-seq inflammatory gene signals를 포함한다.

Investigation note:  
2점 이상 요건인 publication evidence는 충족한다. 실제로는 mechanistic consistency가 강해 3점이다. 단, published prototype과 final ILM01 clinical candidate가 동일하거나 충분히 동등한지는 회사 DD가 필요하다.

---

### 4.4 Platform Attractiveness

Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
GAIA는 차별성이 분명한 biologic fusion platform이다. 다만 SKBP 선호 major modality인 small molecule / ASO / siRNA가 아니라 CMC와 delivery complexity가 큰 biologic이므로 2점이다.

What was checked:
- Is the platform real and reproducible?
- Is differentiation supported by data?
- Does modality fit SKBP priorities?
- Preferred modalities: small molecule, ASO, siRNA
- Secondary modalities: AOC, antibody, biologic

Evidence trail:
- Illimis는 GAIA를 amyloid target binder와 engineered Gas6 TAM receptor binder를 결합한 bispecific fusion protein으로 설명한다.
- 회사는 FcγR-mediated phagocytosis의 inflammatory response를 TAM receptor biology로 우회하는 전략을 제시한다.
- KBR 인터뷰에서는 CMC가 글로벌 임상 진입의 중요 허들이며 내부 CMC 역량을 강화 중이라고 설명한다.

Investigation note:  
2점 이상은 충분하다. 하지만 final candidate manufacturability, immunogenicity, stability, dosing route가 공개되지 않았기 때문에 3점은 보류한다.

---

### 4.5 Expansion Potential

Score: **3 / 3**

Main line:  
ILM01은 one-off AD asset이 아니라 **TAM biology / anti-inflammatory clearance platform**의 lead program으로 보인다.

What was checked:
- Expansion beyond main indication
- Same biology/platform reuse
- Adjacent indications
- Multiple assets from same platform

Evidence trail:
- 공식 pipeline에는 ILM01 외 ILM02 tau/tauopathies, ILM21 myelin debris/MS, ILM24 PS/SLE가 기재되어 있다.
- 공식 platform page는 Aβ, tau, α-syn 후보와 amyloidosis 및 기타 neurodegenerative disease 확장을 언급한다.
- KBR 인터뷰는 ILM02 tauopathy, ILM22 TNF-α/IBD, ILM21 MS myelin debris removal로 확장 중이라고 설명한다.

Investigation note:  
Adjacent indication이 단순 AD 내 세부 환자군이 아니라 tauopathy, MS, SLE/immune disease까지 확장되므로 3점이다.

---

### 4.6 Data Maturity

Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
전임상 PoC는 publication-backed로 강하지만, final ILM01의 IND-enabling package는 공개자료만으로는 확인되지 않는다.

What was checked:
- In vitro data
- In vivo data
- Quantitative result
- Reproducibility
- IND-enabling / GLP tox / PK/PD / CMC / human data availability

Evidence trail:
- 논문/KAIST record는 AD/CAA model mice에서 plaque clearance, behavioral outcome, synapse elimination, microhemorrhage 관련 결과를 제시한다.
- Poster는 in vitro HMC3, THP-1Axl, primary microglia/astrocyte, 5XFAD in vivo, scRNA-seq readout을 포함한다.
- BioSpectator는 ILM01이 2025년 하반기 preclinical stage entry, 2027년 말 IND submission expectation이라고 보도했다.

Investigation note:  
Data package가 “실험 데이터 거의 없음”은 아니다. 그러나 GLP tox, CMC, PK/PD, immunogenicity, human biomarker strategy가 미공개라 3점은 어렵다.

---

### 4.7 Marketability

Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
A. TAP는 미국 AD 7.4M pool에서 diagnosis/eligibility/treatable assumptions를 적용해 약 **46만 명**으로 설정했다. B. Unrisked Peak Sales는 Leqembi/Kisunla price proxy 적용 시 약 **$1.94B**. C. Obtainable Peak Sales는 Leqembi/Kisunla/remternetug competition haircut을 반영해 약 **$1.06B**로 추정했다.

What was checked:
- Targetable addressable patient
- Unrisked peak sales
- Competition haircut
- Pricing power adjustment
- Expansion capacity adjustment

Worksheet:

| Step | What to fill | Evidence / assumption |
|---|---|---|
| A. TAP | 7.4M × 50% diagnosis × 25% eligibility × 50% treatable subgroup = **462,500** | Alzheimer’s Association은 2026년 미국 65세 이상 AD 환자 7.4M을 추정. AUR eligibility는 Korean memory clinic study에서 A+ participants 중 lecanemab 24.6%, donanemab 28.0% proxy 사용 |
| B. Unrisked Peak Sales | 462,500 × $28,000 × 15% × 1.0 = **$1.94B** | Leqembi WAC $26,500/year, Kisunla 약 $32,000/year를 기반으로 $28,000 price proxy 사용 |
| Entry-order matrix | 3-player example: 1st ~50%, 2nd ~30%, 3rd ~20%. ILM01은 approved Aβ drugs와 remternetug 이후 late entrant로 15% 가정 | Leqembi/Kisunla approved, remternetug Phase III |
| C. Obtainable Peak Sales | $1.94B × 0.55 competition haircut × 0.90 pricing power × 1.10 expansion capacity = **$1.06B** | AD market은 크지만 late entrant haircut 필요. ARIA-low/ARIA-free 입증 시 upside 존재 |
| Final score basis | Obtainable peak sales가 $1B 이상 가능하나 $2B 이상은 임상 safety differentiation 전에는 보수적으로 보기 어려움 | **2 / 3** |

Investigation note:
- Marketability는 **rNPV가 아니라 Obtainable Peak Sales** 기준으로 산정했다.
- ILM01이 임상에서 ARIA-free 또는 clearly lower-ARIA profile을 증명하면 pricing power와 competition haircut 모두 개선될 수 있다.

---

## 5) Validation Notes

Cross-checked facts:
- ILM01이 Aβ / Alzheimer’s disease pipeline이라는 점은 official pipeline과 stage/news source로 cross-check했다.
- GAIA mechanism은 official platform, AD Moonshot page, Nature Medicine/KAIST record, poster로 cross-check했다.
- Competitor landscape는 FDA Leqembi/Kisunla approval, Lilly remternetug trial page, Biogen Aduhelm discontinuation으로 확인했다.

Uncertain points:
- Final ILM01 construct가 Nature Medicine αAβ-Gas6 prototype과 어느 정도 동일한지 불명확하다.
- GLP tox, CMC, PK/PD, immunogenicity, human biomarker strategy는 공개자료만으로 확인되지 않는다.
- ClinicalTrials.gov 등 공공 registry에서 ILM01 임상시험 등록은 확인하지 못했다.
- 2027 IND/Phase 1 timeline은 기사/회사성 자료 기반이며, 최신 company deck 또는 직접 확인이 필요하다.

- Financing / partnership sources: [11], [12], [13]

---

## 6) Final Take

One-line summary:  
**ILM01은 “Aβ 제거제”라기보다 “Neuroimmune clearance platform의 lead asset”으로 보는 것이 맞고, SKBP 관점에서는 Watch / DD priority로 관리할 가치가 높다.**

Recommendation:
- **Watch**
- 단, CMC/GLP/PKPD/ARIA translational package가 확보되면 **Shortlist 전환 가능**

Most important diligence question:  
**ILM01 final candidate가 사람에서 기존 anti-Aβ antibody 대비 동등한 amyloid clearance와 의미 있게 낮은 ARIA/neuroinflammation을 동시에 입증할 수 있는가?**

# Hangzhou Westan Pharmaceutical Technology Pipeline Scout Report: **WT-1108**

본 리포트는 SKBP Pipeline Finder v3.0 검토용으로 작성되었으며, auditability 확보를 위해 모든 핵심 판단에 URL 기반 근거를 포함했습니다. 작성 포맷은 업로드된 SKBP Pipeline Finder 지침을 따랐습니다. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

---

## 1) Company Profile

| Country | China | Company profile [10], [11] |
| Headquarters | Hangzhou, Zhejiang, China | VBData company profile [11] |

| Company type / stage | Private clinical-stage biotech focused on small-molecule respiratory / immune disease assets | Official site [1], ByDrug/PharmCube profile [10] |
| Focus areas | Respiratory diseases, immune-related diseases, refractory chronic cough, asthma, pruritus, pain | Official site [1], ByDrug/PharmCube profile [10], BioDuro-Sundia/PharmCube article [8] |
| Platform summary | Company/partner sources describe proprietary compound library + AI-assisted rational drug design platform named WestanAI+. Public reproducibility data were not found. | ByDrug/PharmCube profile [10], BioDuro-Sundia/PharmCube article [8] |
| Financing / partnership signals | ByDrug/PharmCube reports angel and Pre-A financing in tens of millions RMB. WT-1108 was selected for Zhejiang Province “尖兵”“领雁” R&D program. | ByDrug/PharmCube profile [10], Westan news [4] |
| Lead pipeline summary | WT-1108 is a P2X3 antagonist for refractory or unexplained chronic cough. A China Phase IIa RCC/UCC study, CTR20254063, is visible through registry mirror / intelligence sources. | Westan product page [2], Changbaicao trial mirror [6], Synapse [5] |

---

## 2) Pipeline Snapshot

| Field | Content | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Company | Hangzhou Westan Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. | Official website [1], company profile [10] |
| Lead asset | WT-1108 / WT 1108 / WT1108 | Westan product page [2], Synapse [5] |
| Target | P2X3 receptor / P2RX3 | Westan product page [2], Synapse [5] |
| Theme / Cluster | Theme: **No Theme** / Cluster: **No mapped SKBP cluster** | Internal SKBP mapping + target biology sources [12], [32] |
| MoA | Selective P2X3 receptor antagonism to reduce ATP-mediated airway sensory nerve activation and cough hypersensitivity | P2X3 mechanism sources [12], [16], [32] |
| Modality / Platform | Oral small molecule tablet; reportedly developed using WestanAI+ / proprietary compound library | Trial page [6], company / partner sources [8], [10] |
| Indication | Refractory or unexplained chronic cough, RCC/UCC | Westan product page [2], trial page [6] |
| Stage | Phase IIa registered / ongoing; Changbaicao registry mirror lists CTR20254063 as ongoing / not yet recruiting as of 2025-10-11. Synapse lists Phase I CTR20233488 as completed and highest phase as Phase 2. | Changbaicao [6], Synapse [5], Sina [7] |
| Key data | Phase IIa design: randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, 100 mg QD for 4 weeks, target enrollment 48, primary endpoint change in 24h average hourly cough frequency at Week 4. No public WT-1108 efficacy result was found. | Changbaicao [6] |

---

## 3) Scorecard Summary

| Criterion | Score | One-line judgment | Evidence used |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Target Relevance | 1 / 3 | P2X3 is strong cough biology, but weak SKBP CNS E/I or Neuroimmune theme fit. | Westan [2], P2X3 mechanism [32], Merck [16] |
| Competitive Landscape | 1 / 3 | Validated but crowded class: gefapixant approved ex-US, camlipixant Phase III, several China P2X3 followers. | EMA [15], GSK [17], competitor sources [21]-[29] |
| MoA Validity | 2 / 3 | Class MoA is clinically validated, but WT-1108-specific human efficacy is not public. | Gefapixant Phase III [12], [13], camlipixant [20] |
| Platform Attractiveness | 1 / 3 | Oral small molecule is attractive, but WestanAI+ / selectivity / taste-sparing claims lack independent data. | ByDrug [10], BioDuro-Sundia [8], Zhihuiya [9] |
| Expansion Potential | 1 / 3 | Pain, pruritus, COPD/IPF cough expansion is plausible but mostly preclinical / unverified. | BioDuro-Sundia [8], Synapse [5] |
| Data Maturity | 1 / 3 | Phase I completed and Phase IIa registered, but no public WT-1108 outcome package. | Synapse [5], Changbaicao [6] |
| Marketability | 1 / 3 | A. TAP ~1.10M China-addressable patients; B. Unrisked Peak Sales ~USD 148.8M; C. Obtainable Peak Sales ~USD 72.3M. | BioDuro-Sundia [8], GSK [17], DelveInsight [30] |
| **Total** | **8 / 21** | Watch-level asset: validated class, but low SKBP fit and crowded landscape. | Integrated evidence |

---

## 4) Criterion Detail Pages

### 4.1 Target Relevance
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
P2X3 is a clinically relevant cough-hypersensitivity target, but WT-1108 does not directly map to SKBP’s E/I Balance or Neuroimmune themes.

What was checked:
- Target identity
- Disease/biology relevance
- SKBP Theme / Cluster fit
- General neurodegeneration / neuroinflammation / epilepsy relevance

Evidence trail:
- Westan product-page search evidence and Synapse identify WT-1108 as a P2X3 antagonist for RCC/UCC [2], [5].
- Mechanistic sources describe ATP-mediated P2X3 activation in airway sensory nerves as a cough-hypersensitivity pathway [16], [32].
- P2X3 is an ion-channel target, but the relevant biology is mainly peripheral sensory-neuron / airway cough reflex rather than CNS E/I Balance, epilepsy, neurodegeneration, or core neuroimmune biology.
- Pain and pruritus adjacency is mentioned in company / database sources, but no public WT-1108 clinical evidence in those indications was found [5], [8].

Investigation note:
- 1점으로 둔 이유는 respiratory / peripheral sensory indication의 target validity는 높지만, SKBP Pipeline Finder의 CNS-focused Theme / Cluster에는 직접 매핑되지 않기 때문입니다.
- 0점은 아님: P2X3는 ion channel이고 sensory-neuron pain/pruritus adjacency가 있어 peripheral neurobiology 관점의 optional relevance는 존재합니다.

Uncertain points:
- WT-1108의 CNS exposure, CNS indication strategy, neuroimmune relevance는 공개 자료에서 확인되지 않았습니다.
- Pain/pruritus expansion은 공식 임상 개발계획 또는 translational package 확인이 필요합니다.

---

### 4.2 Competitive Landscape
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
P2X3 chronic cough class는 이미 validated 되었지만, WT-1108은 approved / Phase III / China-local Phase I-II competitors 뒤에 위치하는 late-follower risk가 큽니다.

What was checked:
- Same disease competitors
- Same target competitors
- Same or similar MoA competitors
- Front runner count
- Approved / Phase 3 / clinical / preclinical status

Competitor table:

| Competitor | Company | Modality | Target / MoA | Stage | Why it matters | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gefapixant / Lyfnua | Merck & Co. / MSD | Oral small molecule | P2X3 antagonist | Approved in Japan and EU-related markets; US regulatory setback / CRL history | First-in-class benchmark; validates P2X3 but highlights taste disturbance and modest-efficacy risk | EMA [15], Merck [14], Pharmaphorum [33] |
| Camlipixant / BLU-5937 | GSK / BELLUS Health | Oral small molecule | Selective P2X3 antagonist | Phase III CALM-1 / CALM-2 | Most important late-stage best-in-class competitor; GSK acquired BELLUS for ~USD 2B | GSK [17], ClinicalTrials [18], [19], PubMed [20] |
| Sivopixant / S-600918 | Shionogi | Oral small molecule | P2X3 antagonist | Phase II data published; current cough-program status uncertain | Same-MoA clinical comparator; Phase 2b efficacy was not clearly compelling | PubMed [21], Springer [22] |
| HRS-2261 | Hengrui | Oral small molecule | P2X3 antagonist | Phase I published; China Phase II registry visible; current status uncertain | China-local same-target competitor; published Phase I safety/PK benchmark | PubMed [23], Changbaicao [24], PharmCube uncertainty [34] |
| HS-10383 | Hansoh Pharma | Oral small molecule | P2X3 antagonist | Phase I positive MAD data announced; Phase II dose optimization reported | China-local once-daily competitor with no taste-related AE claim in company announcement | Hansoh [25], ClinicalTrials [26] |
| QR052107B / relicpixant | Createrna / Langlai Technology | Oral small molecule | P2X3 antagonist | Phase I positive top-line; Phase II initiated for UCC/RCC per company announcement | Another China-origin P2X3 entrant; reported no taste alteration in healthy volunteers | PRNewswire [27], Pharmacodia [28], Createrna [29] |

Investigation note:
- Same disease and same biology were checked first. The true same-MoA front runners are gefapixant and camlipixant.
- China-local competitors are especially important because WT-1108 is currently a China-origin clinical asset.
- Score is 1 because a high competitor count reduces white-space attractiveness. A higher score would require visible WT-1108 differentiation such as best-in-class taste profile, once-daily convenience, clearly superior cough-count reduction, or broader indication strategy.

Uncertain points:
- HRS-2261 active/discontinued status is unclear; a PharmCube report suggests possible exit, but official sponsor confirmation is needed [34].
- WT-1108 has no public head-to-head data versus gefapixant, camlipixant, HS-10383, HRS-2261, or QR052107B.

---

### 4.3 MoA Validity
Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
P2X3 antagonism has strong class-level clinical validation, but WT-1108-specific efficacy has not yet been publicly shown.

What was checked:
- Journal publication / PMID / DOI
- Mechanistic consistency
- Functional readout
- Disease linkage
- Safety-relevant signal

Evidence trail:
- Gefapixant COUGH-1 / COUGH-2 Phase III trials provide clinical validation for P2X3 antagonism in refractory or unexplained chronic cough [12].
- Pooled Phase III analysis also supports objective cough-frequency and patient-reported outcome improvements for gefapixant, though taste-related adverse events remain class-relevant [13].
- Merck / regulatory materials describe P2X3 receptor activity on sensory nerve fibers and ATP-mediated cough hypersensitivity [16].
- Camlipixant Phase 2b data further supports the mechanism with another selective P2X3 antagonist [20].
- WT-1108 itself has a Phase IIa PoC design with objective 24h cough-frequency endpoint, but no public efficacy readout was found [6].

Investigation note:
- 2점 이상으로 평가 가능한 이유는 publication / regulatory-grade class evidence가 존재하기 때문입니다.
- 3점이 아닌 이유는 WT-1108-specific publication, IC50/selectivity data, Phase I data, Phase IIa efficacy data가 공개되어 있지 않기 때문입니다.

Uncertain points:
- WT-1108의 P2X3 vs P2X2/3 selectivity, IC50, off-target profile, in vivo cough model 결과가 필요합니다.
- Taste disturbance risk를 낮춘다는 주장은 company / partner source 기반이며, human data로 검증되지 않았습니다 [8], [9].

---

### 4.4 Platform Attractiveness
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
Small molecule modality는 SKBP 관점에서 다루기 좋지만, WestanAI+ 플랫폼과 WT-1108 scaffold/selectivity 차별성은 공개 근거가 약합니다.

What was checked:
- Is the platform real and reproducible?
- Is differentiation supported by data?
- Does modality fit SKBP priorities?
- Preferred modalities: small molecule, ASO, siRNA
- Secondary modalities: AOC, antibody, biologic

Evidence trail:
- ByDrug/PharmCube profile describes Westan as using proprietary compound library, AI-assisted drug discovery and rational drug design [10].
- BioDuro-Sundia/PharmCube article states WT-1108 was developed using WestanAI+, has a novel scaffold, high subtype selectivity, strong activity, high stability/bioavailability and low taste side effect potential [8].
- Zhihuiya repeats similar claims about WestanAI+ and WT-1108 differentiation [9].
- No peer-reviewed platform validation, reproducible benchmark, disclosed chemical scaffold, patent-to-data linkage, or quantitative selectivity package was found.

Investigation note:
- 1점으로 둔 이유는 modality 자체는 attractive하지만, platform differentiation이 self/partner-reported 수준에 머물기 때문입니다.
- 2점 이상을 주려면 P2X3 vs P2X2/3 selectivity, oral PK, taste-translational assay, animal cough efficacy, patent family 등 explicit data가 필요합니다.

Uncertain points:
- WestanAI+가 실제로 discovery productivity를 개선했는지 외부 검증자료가 없습니다.
- WT-1108의 exact scaffold, patent coverage, CMC tractability는 미확인입니다.

---

### 4.5 Expansion Potential
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
RCC/UCC 외 pruritus, pain, COPD/IPF cough 확장 가능성은 있으나 대부분 preclinical / concept-level입니다.

What was checked:
- Expansion beyond main indication
- Same biology/platform reuse
- Adjacent indications
- Multiple assets from same platform

Evidence trail:
- BioDuro-Sundia/PharmCube article reports potential follow-on indications including chronic cough subtypes, COPD, pulmonary fibrosis, pruritus and pain [8].
- Synapse lists pain as an active indication and pruritus as preclinical / related status for WT-1108 [5].
- Westan company profile suggests broader respiratory / immune-related disease focus and multiple pipeline assets [1], [10].
- No public WT-1108 clinical study beyond RCC/UCC was found.

Investigation note:
- Adjacent indication은 단순히 “chronic cough” wording change가 아니라 RCC/UCC 외 disease context를 의미합니다.
- 현재 expansion evidence는 mostly company claim / database signal이므로 1점입니다.

Uncertain points:
- Pain/pruritus에서 P2X3 biology가 WT-1108로 충분히 translatable한지 확인 필요합니다.
- COPD/IPF cough의 regulatory path, endpoint, trial population 정의가 필요합니다.

---

### 4.6 Data Maturity
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
Phase I completion and Phase IIa registration은 확인되지만, WT-1108-specific outcome data는 공개되어 있지 않습니다.

What was checked:
- In vitro data
- In vivo data
- Quantitative result
- Reproducibility
- IND-enabling / GLP tox / PK/PD / CMC / human data availability

Evidence trail:
- Synapse lists Phase I CTR20233488 as completed and Phase II CTR20254063 as not yet recruiting / Phase 2 [5].
- Changbaicao registry mirror describes Phase IIa design: randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, 100 mg QD, 4-week treatment, n=48, primary endpoint 24h average hourly cough frequency change at Week 4 [6].
- Sina reports Phase IIa registration / launch context, but this remains secondary news evidence [7].
- No WT-1108 Phase I safety table, PK result, food-effect result, dysgeusia result, in vitro IC50, selectivity panel, animal efficacy model, GLP tox, CMC or human efficacy readout was found.

Investigation note:
- This score is driven by visible experimental evidence, not by stage label alone.
- Phase IIa registration increases confidence that IND-enabling work occurred, but without data disclosure it does not support a 2점 maturity score.

Uncertain points:
- Need Phase I CSR/poster/publication for safety, PK and taste adverse events.
- Need preclinical pharmacology package and translational cough-model data.
- Need official ChinaDrugTrials / CDE direct confirmation for current recruitment and dosing status.

---

### 4.7 Marketability
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
China-addressable RCC/UCC opportunity exists, but WT-1108’s obtainable peak sales is heavily reduced by class crowding, uncertain pricing and late-entry risk.

What was checked:
- Targetable addressable patient
- Unrisked peak sales
- Competition haircut
- Pricing power adjustment
- Expansion capacity adjustment

Worksheet:

| Step | What to fill | Evidence / assumption |
|---|---|---|
| A. TAP | Total Patient Pool x Diagnosis Rate x Eligibility Rate x Treatable Subgroup Rate = 7,000,000 x 45% x 50% x 70% = **1,102,500 patients** | China RCC/UCC patient pool above 7M reported by BioDuro-Sundia/PharmCube [8]. Diagnosis / eligibility / treatable subgroup rates are analyst assumptions and require payer / epidemiology validation. GSK reports 10M global and 6M US/EU RCC >1 year as burden cross-check [17]. |
| B. Unrisked Peak Sales | TAP x Annual Net Price x Peak Penetration x Treatment Duration Factor = 1,102,500 x USD 1,200 x 15% x 75% = **USD 148.8M** | Annual net price is an analyst assumption because no WT-1108 price exists. Peak penetration is capped at 15% due multiple P2X3 competitors. 75% duration factor reflects chronic therapy but tolerability / access uncertainty. |
| Entry-order matrix | 3-player example: 1st ~50%, 2nd ~30%, 3rd ~20% | WT-1108 is modeled as late follower unless it shows best-in-class taste / efficacy. Competitor count: at least 6 same-target assets in approved / Phase III / Phase I-II status [14]-[29]. |
| C. Obtainable Peak Sales | Unrisked Peak Sales x Competition Haircut x Pricing Power Adjustment x Expansion Capacity Adjustment = USD 148.8M x 45% x 90% x 120% = **USD 72.3M** | Competition haircut reflects gefapixant, camlipixant and China-local P2X3 assets. Pricing adjustment reflects uncertain China reimbursement. Expansion capacity 120% reflects unverified pruritus/pain/COPD/IPF optionality [8]. |
| Final score basis | 0 < weak market, 1 <1B obtainable, 2 >=1B, 3 >=2B + high expansion | Obtainable Peak Sales is below USD 1B, so score = 1. |

Investigation note:
- Marketability is based on obtainable peak sales, not rNPV.
- Calculation uses a conservative China-focused base because WT-1108 is a China-origin Phase IIa asset and no verified global licensing / commercial rights package was found.
- If WT-1108 obtains global rights coverage and shows best-in-class human taste/tolerability and efficacy versus camlipixant / gefapixant, obtainable peak sales could be materially higher.

Uncertain points:
- No verified WT-1108 annual net price, payer coverage or reimbursement assumption was found.
- China-specific RCC diagnosis rate and eligible moderate/severe treatable rate require epidemiology validation.
- Global market opportunity needs separate US/EU/Japan/Korea regulatory and partnering analysis.

## 5) Validation Notes

- Market / epidemiology sources: BioDuro-Sundia/PharmCube [8], GSK/BELLUS [17], DelveInsight [30], GSK burden page [31]
- Financing / partnership sources: ByDrug/PharmCube [10], Westan news [4], BioDuro-Sundia/PharmCube [8]

## 6) Final Take

One-line summary:  
WT-1108은 clinically staged P2X3 chronic cough asset으로 class validation은 강하지만, SKBP CNS-theme fit이 낮고 P2X3 경쟁이 과밀하여 현재는 Shortlist보다 Watch가 적절합니다.

Recommendation:
- **Watch**

Most important diligence question:  
Can Westan provide WT-1108-specific human Phase I / early Phase II evidence showing best-in-class efficacy, low taste disturbance, clean PK/safety and a credible global partnering path versus camlipixant, gefapixant and China-local P2X3 followers?

## 4) Criterion Detail Pages

### 4.1 Target Relevance
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
UX-EP001 is associated with epilepsy, which is highly relevant to SKBP’s E/I Balance theme, but the target and disease-modifying hypothesis are not disclosed for the exact asset.

What was checked:
- Target identity
- Disease/biology relevance
- SKBP Theme / Cluster fit
- General neurodegeneration / neuroinflammation / epilepsy relevance

Evidence trail:
- Synapse lists UX-EP001 as an asset from Shanghai Yuesai Biotechnology with active indication epilepsy, therapeutic area nervous system diseases, and highest phase preclinical [7].
- Synapse does not list a target, action, or mechanism for UX-EP001 [7].
- UniXell’s official epilepsy program UX-GIP001 targets drug-resistant epilepsy by differentiating iPSCs into inhibitory neuronal progenitor cells to restore GABAergic circuitry [4], [6].
- This GABAergic interneuron hypothesis strongly maps to E/I Balance and Network Modulation, but it is not safe to transfer that MoA to UX-EP001 unless the company confirms UX-EP001 = UX-GIP001 or a related program.

Investigation note:
- 1점으로 둔 이유는 epilepsy indication itself는 SKBP CNS/E-I theme relevance가 있지만, exact UX-EP001 target/MoA가 비공개이기 때문입니다.
- 2점 이상은 target or circuit mechanism이 asset-specific으로 확인될 때 가능함.

Uncertain points:
- UXEP-001이 Synapse의 UX-EP001과 동일한지 확인 필요.
- UX-EP001이 UniXell 공식 epilepsy program UX-GIP001의 구명/오타/이전 코드인지 확인 필요.
- Molecular or cellular target, delivery route, cell source, differentiation lineage가 모두 미공개.

### 4.2 Competitive Landscape
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
UX-EP001은 exact data가 없는 preclinical asset인 반면, 같은 epilepsy disease-modifying/cell therapy field에는 이미 clinical-stage front-runner가 존재합니다.

What was checked:
- Same disease competitors
- Same target competitors
- Same or similar MoA competitors
- Front runner count
- Approved / Phase 3 / clinical / preclinical status

Competitor table:

| Competitor | Company | Modality | Target / MoA | Stage | Why it matters | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| NRTX-1001 / rezanecel | Neurona Therapeutics / UCB | Allogeneic GABAergic interneuron cell therapy | Human pluripotent stem cell-derived inhibitory interneurons; local GABAergic circuit repair in MTLE | Phase I/II; UCB acquisition announced; Phase III EPIC planned/initiating in 2026 | Most important same-disease, same-regenerative-therapy benchmark; FDA RMAT and EMA PRIME reported | UCB [11], Neurona [12], ClinicalTrials [13], AES [14] |
| UX-GIP001 | UniXell Biotech | iPSC-derived allogeneic GABAergic interneuron progenitor cell therapy | Restores GABAergic inhibitory circuitry in drug-resistant focal epilepsy / MTLE | IND cleared in U.S. and China; Phase I / early Phase I records visible | Same company’s official epilepsy program; may be the asset actually intended by UXEP-001 input | Official UniXell [4], PRNewswire/BioSpace [6], ClinicalTrials [8], [9] |
| ETX101 | Encoded Therapeutics | AAV9 gene regulation therapy | Upregulates SCN1A expression in GABAergic inhibitory neurons for SCN1A+ Dravet syndrome | Phase I/II; RMAT / Breakthrough designations and pivotal alignment reported | Disease-modifying epilepsy competitor, but different genetic epilepsy segment | Encoded [15], ClinicalTrials [16] |
| Zorevunersen / STK-001 | Stoke Therapeutics / Biogen | Antisense oligonucleotide | Increases functional NaV1.1 protein from wild-type SCN1A copy in Dravet syndrome | Phase III underway / planned; NEJM Phase 1/2 data published | Strong precision-medicine epilepsy competitor; not same MTLE segment but raises DMT bar | NEJM/PubMed [19], Stoke [20], Dravet Foundation [21] |
| Approved antiseizure medicines / cenobamate class | SK Life Science and others | Small molecule ASM | Symptomatic seizure suppression via ion-channel / neurotransmission mechanisms | Approved for focal seizures | Current standard-of-care comparator; not disease-modifying, but high commercial and clinical baseline | FDA label / approved ASM sources needed; not used for direct scoring due no specific source retrieved |
| Epilepsy surgery / RNS / DBS | Device/procedure competitors | Surgery / neuromodulation | Resective/ablative or electrical modulation of seizure networks | Established care for selected drug-resistant focal epilepsy | Non-drug competition for focal epilepsy; cell therapy must show safety and durable seizure reduction vs surgical/device options | Clinical guideline/source needed; not scored as drug asset |

Investigation note:
- 경쟁분석은 “same disease + disease-modifying modality” 기준으로 먼저 정리했습니다.
- UX-EP001의 target/MoA가 미공개라 same-target competitor는 정의 불가합니다.
- NRTX-1001과 UX-GIP001은 GABAergic interneuron replacement/circuit repair라는 측면에서 가장 중요한 비교군입니다.
- Score는 1점: category attractiveness는 존재하나, exact asset은 preclinical/undisclosed이고 front-runner 대비 후행 리스크가 큽니다.

Uncertain points:
- UX-EP001과 UX-GIP001의 관계가 불명확합니다.
- UX-EP001이 정말 TCR-T cell therapy라면 위 competitor set은 disease-level competitor이며, same-modality competitor는 확인되지 않았습니다.
- Exact patient segment, route, MoA가 확인되어야 competitor mapping을 재정의할 수 있습니다.

### 4.3 MoA Validity
Score: **0 / 3**

Main line:  
UX-EP001-specific MoA evidence가 공개되어 있지 않으며, Synapse의 “TCR-T Cell therapy” 표기는 epilepsy indication과 생물학적으로 부자연스러워 검증 전까지 scoring에 사용하기 어렵습니다.

What was checked:
- Journal publication / PMID / DOI
- Mechanistic consistency
- Functional readout
- Disease linkage
- Safety-relevant signal

Evidence trail:
- Synapse lists UX-EP001 as TCR-T cell therapy, but target/action/mechanism fields are empty [7].
- No UX-EP001 publication, patent, company page, preclinical abstract, poster, registry, or IND source was found.
- UniXell’s official UX-GIP001 MoA is mechanistically coherent: impaired GABAergic interneurons drive hyperexcitability, and iPSC-derived inhibitory progenitors aim to reconstruct inhibitory circuits [4], [6].
- However, UX-GIP001 evidence cannot be used as UX-EP001-specific MoA unless asset identity is confirmed.

Investigation note:
- 0점으로 둔 이유는 exact asset에 대한 MoA가 비공개이고, 공개 데이터가 asset-specific validity를 뒷받침하지 못하기 때문입니다.
- 만약 회사가 UX-EP001 = UX-GIP001이라고 확인하면 MoA Validity는 2점까지 상향 가능성이 있습니다.

Uncertain points:
- UX-EP001의 exact modality, cellular product identity, target cell population, delivery site, mechanism, animal efficacy readout가 모두 필요합니다.
- Synapse의 TCR-T label 오류 가능성을 확인해야 합니다.

### 4.4 Platform Attractiveness
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
UniXell의 hPSC/iPSC neural cell therapy platform은 공개자료상 credible하지만, UX-EP001이 이 platform에서 나온 어떤 product인지 확인되지 않습니다.

What was checked:
- Is the platform real and reproducible?
- Is differentiation supported by data?
- Does modality fit SKBP priorities?
- Preferred modalities: small molecule, ASO, siRNA
- Secondary modalities: AOC, antibody, biologic

Evidence trail:
- UniXell official page states the company has reprogramming technology, stem cell differentiation, SISBAR lineage tracing, and high-precision gene editing technologies [2], [3].
- Company source states the platform supports high-purity and batch-consistent cell products [2].
- BioSpace/PRNewswire describes 4,000 m² R&D/GMP facilities and hPSC/iPSC neural cell therapy platform capabilities [6].
- Published academic work associated with the founder/platform includes SISBAR lineage tracing and SphereDiff midbrain dopaminergic progenitor differentiation, but these publications are more directly connected to platform science and UX-DA001-type dopaminergic products, not necessarily UX-EP001 [22], [23].
- SKBP preference is generally small molecule/ASO/siRNA first; complex intracranial cell therapy is strategically interesting but outside easy-fit modality.

Investigation note:
- 1점으로 둔 이유는 platform credibility는 있으나 exact UX-EP001 differentiation lineage, CMC method, release criteria, potency assay, and safety package가 공개되어 있지 않기 때문입니다.
- Cell therapy는 CNS disease-modifying potential은 높지만, SKBP operational fit과 BD tractability는 lower than small molecule/ASO입니다.

Uncertain points:
- UX-EP001이 UniXell’s iPSC/GABA progenitor platform asset인지 불명확.
- Need cell source, allogeneic/autologous status, gene editing status, immunosuppression plan, delivery method, potency assay and release criteria.

### 4.5 Expansion Potential
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
Company-level CNS expansion potential은 높지만, UX-EP001-specific expansion은 공개되어 있지 않습니다.

What was checked:
- Expansion beyond main indication
- Same biology/platform reuse
- Adjacent indications
- Multiple assets from same platform

Evidence trail:
- UniXell official pipeline includes multiple CNS cell therapy programs: UX-DA001/UX-DA002 for Parkinson’s disease, UX-GIP001 for epilepsy, UX-HE001 for hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy, UX-SP001 for spinal cord injury, and undisclosed programs [3].
- Official news states UniXell will continue advancing multiple neurological cell therapy pipelines and has built both autologous and allogeneic strategies [4], [6].
- Synapse organization page lists eight drug assets and multiple neurological disease areas [5].
- UX-EP001 itself only has epilepsy listed, with no adjacent indication or platform extension data [7].

Investigation note:
- 1점으로 둔 이유는 company/platform expansion은 가능하지만 asset-specific expansion은 확인되지 않기 때문입니다.
- Adjacent indication은 same disease wording change가 아니라, MTLE 외 focal epilepsy subtype, HIE, brain injury, or other network disorders로의 확장을 의미합니다.

Uncertain points:
- UX-EP001의 product identity 확인 전까지 expansion hypothesis cannot be trusted.
- If UX-EP001 is separate from UX-GIP001, its disease scope may be narrower or entirely different.

## 5) Validation Notes

Cross-checked facts:
- UX-EP001 exact match appears in Synapse as a Shanghai Yuesai / UniXell preclinical epilepsy asset, but not on UniXell official pipeline [7], [3].
- UniXell official pipeline confirms UX-GIP001 as the visible epilepsy product: GABA progenitor cells for epilepsy [3], [4].
- UX-GIP001 has U.S. and China IND clearance according to UniXell/PRNewswire/BioSpace, and ClinicalTrials.gov records exist for UX-GIP001 studies [4], [6], [8], [9].
- NRTX-1001 is the most advanced comparable GABAergic interneuron cell therapy competitor; UCB announced acquisition of Neurona with up to USD 1.15B total transaction value and described Phase I/II status, RMAT and PRIME designations [11].
- Disease burden: UniXell sources state 70M global epilepsy patients, 10M in China, and ~30% drug-resistant; WHO gives a more conservative global epilepsy estimate around 50M [4], [6], [18].

Uncertain points:
- **Most important:** confirm whether UXEP-001 = UX-EP001 or UX-GIP001.
- If UX-EP001 is separate from UX-GIP001, official company confirmation is needed for modality, target, MoA, indication, stage and ownership.
- Synapse lists UX-EP001 as “TCR-T Cell therapy,” but no company source corroborates this and the biology is unclear for epilepsy.
- No UX-EP001-specific clinical trial, patent, poster, publication, IND, tox, CMC or animal efficacy data was found.
- Market model is speculative and should not be used for valuation before identity confirmation.

## 6) Final Take

One-line summary:  
**UX-EP001 exact-match 기준으로는 “epilepsy preclinical asset” 외에 target/MoA/data가 공개되지 않아 Deprioritize에 가깝지만, 명칭이 UX-GIP001을 의미한 것이라면 Watch~Shortlist 재평가가 필요합니다.**

Recommendation:
- **Deprioritize for exact UX-EP001**
- **Re-check / Watch if the intended asset is UX-GIP001**

Most important diligence question:  
Is UXEP-001 a typo/alias for **UX-GIP001**, or is it a separate preclinical epilepsy asset? If separate, what is the actual modality, cell identity, target/MoA, delivery route, preclinical efficacy package, and ownership status?

# Lynk Pharmaceuticals Pipeline Scout Report: **LNK01006 / BLKR201**

본 리포트는 SKBP Pipeline Finder v3.0 검토용으로 작성되었으며, auditability 확보를 위해 모든 핵심 판단에 URL 기반 근거를 포함했습니다. 작성 포맷은 업로드된 SKBP Pipeline Finder 지침을 따랐습니다. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

**Name-resolution note:** 유저 입력은 `LYNK01006`입니다. 공개자료상 정확한 asset 표기는 **LNK01006 / LNK-01006**, Formation Bio 개발명은 **BLKR201 / BLKR-201**입니다. 본 리포트는 Lynk Pharmaceuticals가 발굴하고 Formation Bio/Bleecker Bio가 Greater China 제외 worldwide rights를 확보한 **CNS-penetrant allosteric TYK2 inhibitor**를 기준으로 작성했습니다.

| Competitive Landscape | 2 / 3 | CNS-penetrant TYK2 is still relatively open, but NEU-627/A-005 and advanced MS BTK competitors create meaningful pressure. | Neuron23 [12], Alumis [13], Roche fenebrutinib [17], Sanofi tolebrutinib [18] |
| MoA Validity | 2 / 3 | TYK2 is clinically validated peripherally and genetically/preclinically supported for CNS neuroinflammation, but LNK01006 has no human efficacy yet. | BMS Sotyktu [14], PNAS [11], Lynk [2] |
| Platform Attractiveness | 2 / 3 | Oral CNS-penetrant selective allosteric small molecule is highly attractive, but LNK01006 quantitative differentiation is not publicly disclosed. | Formation [3], Lynk [2], licensing deal [4] |
| Expansion Potential | 2 / 3 | MS plus central/compartmentalized immune disease optionality is credible, but expansion remains preclinical/hypothesis-stage. | Formation [3], Synapse [8], PNAS [11] |
| Data Maturity | 1 / 3 | IND-cleared and Phase 1 dosed, but no LNK01006-specific human data or public quantitative preclinical package. | ClinicalTrials.gov [7], PRNewswire [5], Synapse [8] |
| Marketability | 2 / 3 | A. TAP ~928k global MS/neuroimmune-addressable patients; B. Unrisked Peak Sales ~USD 2.32B; C. Obtainable Peak Sales ~USD 1.04B. | Atlas of MS [19], National MS Society [20], Ocrevus pricing [21], MS competition [17], [18] |
| **Total** | **14 / 21** | Strong scientific shortlist candidate, but BD availability is constrained by Formation’s ex-Greater China license. | Integrated evidence |

---

## 4) Criterion Detail Pages

### 4.1 Target Relevance
Score: **3 / 3**

Main line:  
LNK01006는 TYK2-mediated cytokine signaling을 CNS에서 조절하려는 oral small molecule로, SKBP Neuroimmune / Cytokine 신경조절 theme에 직접적으로 부합합니다.

What was checked:
- Target identity
- Disease/biology relevance
- SKBP Theme / Cluster fit
- General neurodegeneration / neuroinflammation / epilepsy relevance

Evidence trail:
- Lynk는 LNK01006을 **highly brain-penetrant, potent, selective oral TYK2 allosteric inhibitor targeting the JH2 domain**으로 설명하며, IL-12/IL-23 signaling, Th1/Th17 differentiation, type I interferon-related CNS inflammatory pathways를 언급했습니다 [2].
- Formation Bio는 BLKR201/LNK01006의 CNS exposure와 TYK2 selectivity가 central 또는 compartmentalized immune pathway가 관여하는 autoimmune/inflammatory diseases로 확장될 수 있다고 설명했습니다 [3], [5].
- PNAS 2025 논문은 brain-penetrant TYK2 inhibitors가 EAE/MS neuroinflammation model에서 central TYK2 inhibition의 역할을 확인했다고 보고했습니다 [11].
- Neuron23도 TYK2 loss-of-function/genetics와 MS protection, brain-penetrant TYK2 inhibition rationale을 공식 pipeline에서 설명하고 있습니다 [12].

Investigation note:
- 3점으로 평가한 이유는 target identity가 명확하고, CNS-penetrant TYK2 inhibition이 SKBP Neuroimmune theme에 직접 연결되며, MS/neuroinflammation disease biology와 human genetics / preclinical biology가 모두 visible하기 때문입니다.
- 단, 현재 임상 적응증은 아직 healthy-volunteer Phase 1이므로 disease PoC는 없습니다.

Uncertain points:
- LNK01006의 실제 CNS exposure 수치, CSF/plasma ratio, brain free fraction, species translation은 공개되지 않았습니다.
- MS 중 relapsing MS, progressive MS, PMS, AD/PD neuroinflammation 등 우선 indication은 Formation/Bleecker의 Phase 2 전략 확인이 필요합니다.

---

### 4.2 Competitive Landscape
Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
CNS-penetrant TYK2는 아직 approved asset이 없는 differentiated white space지만, same-MoA Phase 1 competitors와 advanced MS neuroimmune competitors가 빠르게 형성되고 있습니다.

What was checked:
- Same disease competitors
- Same target competitors
- Same or similar MoA competitors
- Front runner count
- Approved / Phase 3 / clinical / preclinical status

Competitor table:

| Competitor | Company | Modality | Target / MoA | Stage | Why it matters | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| NEU-627 | Neuron23 | Oral small molecule | Brain-penetrant TYK2 inhibitor for MS/neuroinflammation | Phase 1 listed; NEU-627 healthy-volunteer study shown as completed in Synapse/Veeva sources | Closest same-target, same-CNS-penetrant TYK2 competitor | Neuron23 [12], Synapse/Veeva [22] |
| A-005 | Alumis | Oral small molecule | CNS-penetrant allosteric TYK2 inhibitor for neuroinflammatory/neurodegenerative diseases, initial MS | Phase 1 positive data announced; development for MS/PD-like CNS indications | Direct CNS-TYK2 competitor with Phase 1 data announcement | Alumis [13] |
| Deucravacitinib / Sotyktu | Bristol Myers Squibb | Oral small molecule | Selective allosteric TYK2 inhibitor; regulatory/JH2 domain | Approved for plaque psoriasis; TYK2 clinical validation benchmark | Validates peripheral allosteric TYK2, but not CNS penetration or MS efficacy | BMS [14] |
| Zasocitinib / TAK-279 | Takeda | Oral small molecule | Highly selective allosteric TYK2 inhibitor | Phase 3 psoriasis; positive head-to-head vs deucravacitinib announced in 2026 | Shows next-gen peripheral TYK2 competition and efficacy bar for selectivity | Takeda [15], JAMA Dermatology [16] |
| Fenebrutinib | Roche | Oral small molecule | BTK inhibitor, B-cell/myeloid CNS-relevant immunomodulation | Phase 3 MS; Roche reported positive RMS/PPMS data and regulatory submission | Not same target, but major oral neuroimmune MS competitor; likely shapes MS market entry | Roche [17] |
| Tolebrutinib | Sanofi | Oral small molecule | Brain-penetrant BTK inhibitor | Phase 3; nrSPMS benefit reported, PPMS failure reported; regulatory status evolving | Important progressive MS / CNS immune benchmark and risk comparator | Sanofi [18] |

Investigation note:
- Same target and same CNS-penetrant class were separated from broader MS competitors.
- NEU-627 and A-005 are the highest-similarity competitors because they share **brain/CNS-penetrant TYK2 inhibition**.
- Fenebrutinib/tolebrutinib are not TYK2 inhibitors but matter commercially because they are advanced oral CNS-immune MS competitors.
- Score is 2 because CNS-TYK2 is differentiated and not yet saturated, but the MS/neuroimmune field is competitive and front-runners are close or ahead.

Uncertain points:
- LNK01006 vs NEU-627 vs A-005 potency, TYK2/JAK selectivity, CNS exposure, CSF PD, safety and dose cannot be compared publicly.
- Formation/Bleecker’s exact disease indication for Phase 2 is not disclosed.
- Greater China rights remain with Lynk; global BD opportunity is already encumbered by Formation.

---

### 4.3 MoA Validity
Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
Allosteric TYK2 inhibition is clinically validated in peripheral immune disease and biologically supported in MS/neuroinflammation, but LNK01006-specific disease efficacy remains preclinical/company-reported.

What was checked:
- Journal publication / PMID / DOI
- Mechanistic consistency
- Functional readout
- Disease linkage
- Safety-relevant signal

Evidence trail:
- Deucravacitinib/Sotyktu approval validates selective allosteric TYK2 inhibition as a druggable immune target in humans [14].
- Lynk states LNK01006 targets TYK2 JH2 and modulates IL-12/IL-23, type I interferon and Th1/Th17-related inflammatory pathways relevant to CNS inflammatory diseases such as MS [2].
- PNAS 2025 central TYK2 inhibition work reported brain-penetrant TYK2 inhibitors modulated EAE/MS-like neuroinflammation and implicated astrocyte/microglial inflammatory biology [11].
- LNK01006 itself is now in first-in-human healthy-volunteer testing, but no disease-patient efficacy readout exists [5], [7].

Investigation note:
- 2점 이상으로 평가 가능한 이유는 target class, biology, genetics and preclinical CNS TYK2 rationale가 visible하기 때문입니다.
- 3점이 아닌 이유는 LNK01006-specific peer-reviewed paper, quantitative preclinical package and clinical disease PoC가 공개되지 않았기 때문입니다.

Uncertain points:
- LNK01006의 EAE model result, dose-response, biomarker modulation, free brain exposure, CSF PD data가 필요합니다.
- TYK2 inhibition이 progressive MS 또는 neurodegeneration에서 실제 clinical efficacy로 이어질지는 아직 검증되지 않았습니다.

---

### 4.4 Platform Attractiveness
Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
Oral, CNS-penetrant, selective allosteric small molecule은 SKBP 관점에서 attractive하지만, LNK01006의 best-in-class 차별성은 아직 company claim 수준입니다.

What was checked:
- Is the platform real and reproducible?
- Is differentiation supported by data?
- Does modality fit SKBP priorities?
- Preferred modalities: small molecule, ASO, siRNA
- Secondary modalities: AOC, antibody, biologic

Evidence trail:
- LNK01006은 small molecule이며, SKBP 선호 modality인 oral small molecule에 해당합니다 [2], [3].
- Formation은 LNK01006이 target selectivity, metabolic stability, CNS penetration을 위해 optimized scaffold로 설계되었다고 설명합니다 [3].
- Lynk는 JAK-STAT/TYK2 small-molecule expertise와 clinical-stage pipeline을 보유하고 있으며, 2023년 Series C funding과 2025년 Formation license deal이 external validation signal로 작동합니다 [4], [9].
- 다만 LNK01006의 chemical structure, selectivity table, kinase panel, hERG/off-target, CYP/transporters, CMC tractability, tox margin은 공개되지 않았습니다.

Investigation note:
- 2점으로 평가한 이유는 modality와 CNS-penetrant allosteric design 자체가 매력적이고 Formation deal validation이 있기 때문입니다.
- 3점은 공개 quantitative differentiation 또는 human CSF/PD data 확인 전에는 어렵습니다.

Uncertain points:
- LNK01006의 full kinase selectivity/JAK1/2/3 sparing profile 필요.
- CNS exposure가 “brain penetrant” claim을 넘어 human CSF PD로 확인되는지 필요.
- Greater China 외 rights는 Formation/Bleecker가 보유하므로 SKBP 접근 가능한 deal structure가 제한될 수 있습니다.

---

### 4.5 Expansion Potential
Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
MS에서 시작해 broader CNS inflammatory / neurodegenerative immune pathway로 확장 가능성이 있으나, 아직 Phase 1 전후 hypothesis stage입니다.

What was checked:
- Expansion beyond main indication
- Same biology/platform reuse
- Adjacent indications
- Multiple assets from same platform

Evidence trail:
- Formation은 BLKR201/LNK01006을 central or compartmentalized immune pathways가 관여하는 autoimmune/inflammatory conditions로 확장할 수 있다고 설명합니다 [3], [5].
- Synapse는 active indication으로 inflammation, multiple sclerosis, Alzheimer disease 등을 표시하고, China 쪽 AD/PD IND application status도 표시하지만, 이는 official disease-stage confirmation이 필요합니다 [8].
- PNAS central TYK2 inhibition work는 MS뿐 아니라 astrocyte/microglia-related neuroinflammation biology를 제시하며, neurodegenerative diseases로의 hypothesis 확장을 뒷받침합니다 [11].
- Formation의 AI-native development model은 indication strategy, evidence generation, trial execution을 빠르게 진행하는 플랫폼으로 포지셔닝됩니다 [5].

Investigation note:
- 2점으로 둔 이유는 biology expansion이 plausible하고 CNS penetration이 platform-like optionality를 주기 때문입니다.
- 3점은 multiple disease-specific preclinical packages 또는 clinical indication selection이 공개되어야 가능합니다.

Uncertain points:
- Formation/Bleecker가 실제로 선택한 first patient indication이 MS인지, 다른 inflammatory indication인지 불명확합니다.
- AD/PD neuroinflammation은 high-risk expansion이며, disease-modifying clinical signal 없이 시장성 반영은 제한적입니다.

---

### 4.6 Data Maturity
Score: **1 / 3**

Main line:  
LNK01006은 IND-cleared, first-in-human Phase 1 dosed asset이지만, public human data는 아직 없습니다.

What was checked:
- In vitro data
- In vivo data
- Quantitative result
- Reproducibility
- IND-enabling / GLP tox / PK/PD / CMC / human data availability

Evidence trail:
- FDA IND clearance for LNK01006 was announced in Dec 2025 [2].
- BLKR201-101-HV Phase 1 is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled SAD/MAD healthy-volunteer study to evaluate safety, tolerability, PK, PD and food effect [7], [8].
- Formation/Lynk announced first participant dosing in Jun 2026 and expected readout in early 2027 [5].
- Company-reported preclinical data include high brain penetration and promising MS model efficacy, but no detailed quantitative package was found [2].
- No LNK01006-specific peer-reviewed paper, Phase 1 results, dose-exposure, CSF biomarker, tox margin or CMC package was found.

Investigation note:
- This score is driven by visible data, not deal value.
- Phase 1 dosing is a major maturity step, but no human safety/PK/PD readout exists yet, so 1점입니다.

Uncertain points:
- Need Phase 1 SAD/MAD safety, tolerability, PK, food effect and PD results.
- Need CNS exposure confirmation in human or translational CSF/brain biomarker package.
- Need IND-enabling tox and kinase selectivity package.

---

### 4.7 Marketability
Score: **2 / 3**

Main line:  
MS/neuroinflammation market is large and oral CNS-penetrant TYK2 could be commercially meaningful, but competition and early-stage risk heavily haircut obtainable sales.

What was checked:
- Targetable addressable patient
- Unrisked peak sales
- Competition haircut
- Pricing power adjustment
- Expansion capacity adjustment

Worksheet:

| Step | What to fill | Evidence / assumption |
|---|---|---|
| A. TAP | Global MS prevalence 2.9M x diagnosis/commercial reach 80% x eligible neuroimmune disease-form rate 80% x treatable subgroup 50% = **928,000 patients** | Atlas of MS reports 2.9M people living with MS in 2023 [19]. National MS Society states ~85% are initially diagnosed with RRMS and PPMS is ~10–15% [20], [23]. Diagnosis/reach and treatable subgroup are analyst assumptions. |
| B. Unrisked Peak Sales | TAP 928,000 x annual net price USD 50,000 x peak penetration 5% x treatment duration factor 1.0 = **USD 2.32B** | Ocrevus list price is USD 78,858 annually; oral small-molecule net price is assumed lower at USD 50,000 [21]. Penetration is capped at 5% due crowded MS market and Phase 1 status. |
| Entry-order matrix | 3-player example: 1st ~50%, 2nd ~30%, 3rd ~20% | In CNS-TYK2, NEU-627/A-005/LNK01006 form an emerging 3-player field; in broader MS, BTK inhibitors and anti-CD20 therapies compress share. |
| C. Obtainable Peak Sales | USD 2.32B x competition haircut 45% x pricing power adjustment 80% x expansion capacity adjustment 125% = **USD 1.044B** | Competition haircut reflects Roche fenebrutinib, Sanofi tolebrutinib, anti-CD20 standards and same-MoA TYK2 competitors [17], [18], [12], [13]. Expansion uplift reflects possible broader central immune diseases [3], [11]. |
| Final score basis | 0 < weak market, 1 <1B obtainable, 2 >=1B, 3 >=2B + high expansion | Obtainable Peak Sales is modeled just above USD 1B, so score = 2. |

Investigation note:
- Marketability is based on obtainable peak sales, not rNPV.
- Formation’s up to USD 605M licensing structure supports external commercial validation but does not itself prove peak sales [4].
- If human CNS PK/PD and Phase 2 efficacy are strong, obtainable peak sales could exceed this base case. If CNS exposure or efficacy is weak, value could fall below USD 1B quickly.

Uncertain points:
- Exact lead patient indication and regulatory positioning are not disclosed.
- Annual net price is assumption-based and should be refined by MS DMT net-price benchmarks and payer research.
- Commercial rights are already licensed ex-Greater China, which may reduce SKBP BD optionality.

## 5) Validation Notes

Cross-checked facts:
- LNK01006 = BLKR201 / BLKR-201 was cross-checked across Formation Bio, Lynk/PRNewswire, Synapse and ClinicalTrials.gov [3], [5], [7], [8].
- FDA IND clearance and first-in-human Phase 1 status were cross-checked using Lynk announcement, Formation first-dose announcement and NCT07501039 [2], [5], [7].

- MS market sizing was cross-checked using Atlas of MS, National MS Society and Ocrevus pricing sources [19], [20], [21].

Uncertain points:
- LNK01006 quantitative potency/selectivity/CNS exposure data not public.
- Human Phase 1 safety/PK/PD data not yet available; readout expected early 2027 per Formation/Lynk [5].
- Formation/Bleecker’s first disease indication after healthy-volunteer Phase 1 is not disclosed.
- Greater China rights remain with Lynk, while worldwide ex-Greater China rights are Formation/Bleecker; SKBP-accessible deal territory requires confirmation.

## 6) Final Take

One-line summary:  
**LNK01006 / BLKR201은 SKBP Neuroimmune 관점에서 매우 매력적인 CNS-penetrant allosteric TYK2 small molecule이며, scientific shortlist 가치가 있으나, Formation Bio가 Greater China 제외 권리를 이미 확보해 BD 접근성은 제한적입니다.**

Recommendation:
- **Shortlist** for scientific diligence
- BD feasibility: **Review / constrained by existing ex-Greater China license**

Most important diligence question:  
Can Lynk/Formation provide quantitative human-translatable evidence that LNK01006 achieves sufficient CNS exposure and TYK2 pathway inhibition at a safe oral dose, and is any territory or collaboration structure still accessible to SKBP after the Formation/Bleecker ex-Greater China license?

final_insight.most_important_diligence_question: Can Lynk/Formation provide quantitative human-translatable evidence that LNK01006 achieves sufficient CNS exposure and TYK2 pathway inhibition at a safe oral dose, and is any territory or collaboration structure still accessible to SKBP after the Formation/Bleecker ex-Greater China license?
